dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 10.45 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Settings → MCP redesigned in Apple style. Connector rows become rounded cards with an SF Symbol, name, and small tinted status pill on top; Tools / Prompt are subtle chevron links instead of bright pills; the tool list drops the zebra stripes for native macOS Settings sectioning — small grey category captions, rounded cards, hairline dividers, and a summary chip "N of M tools enabled · X tokens / message" at the top of the expanded list. Priority badges (Essential, Advanced) are low-saturation tinted capsules. Standard popover only scrolls the account list now. Auto-swap and Token-usage cards are pinned to the bottom of the popover regardless of how many accounts you have. Up to three accounts no scroll at all; four or more scroll inside the account list only. Tiny popover's threshold slider no longer twitches as the digits cross 9→10 or 99→100 — the "current" and "trigger" values render into fixed-width slots so the row stays still during drag. Tiny popover width restored to 290pt — the 227pt squeeze in v10.44 broke the 5h/7d chip text onto multiple lines. Menu-bar popover now floats above the Settings window so picking a layout in Settings → General actually shows the live preview instead of hiding it behind the panel.

Fixed Tiny popover threshold slider jittering on digit-count boundaries during drag. Fixed Tiny popover layout collapsing 5h/7d chip values onto multiple lines at 227pt width. Fixed Standard popover scrolling the entire body (including Auto-swap + Token cards) when more than 3 accounts existed — those bottom cards are now pinned and only the account list scrolls. Fixed live popover preview being hidden behind the Settings window when picking a layout.
